Income.title: Income
Income.description: Represents a single income record for the clinic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the income record.

source.description: Source of the income (e.g., 'Patient payment', 'Insurance reimbursement').

description.description: Optional description or notes about the income.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Represents a single expense record for the clinic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the expense record.

description.description: Optional description or notes about the expense.

vendor.description: Name of the vendor the expense was paid to.

IncomeCategory.title: IncomeCategory
IncomeCategory.description: Represents a category for classifying income sources.

id.description: Unique identifier for the income category.

name.description: Name of the income category (e.g., 'Patient Payments', 'Insurance').

description.description: Optional description of the income category.

ExpenseCategory.title: ExpenseCategory
ExpenseCategory.description: Represents a category for classifying expenses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the expense category.

description.description: Optional description of the expense category.

Budget.title: Budget
Budget.description: Represents a budget for a specific category and time period.

categoryId.description: Reference to either IncomeCategory or ExpenseCategory.  (Relationship: Category 1:N Budget)

startDate.description: Start date of the budget period.

endDate.description: End date of the budget period.

definition.description: Stores income records for a specific user. Each income document is owned by the user identified by {userId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the income record.

params.name: incomeId
params.description: The ID of the income record.

definition.description: Stores expense records for a specific user. Each expense document is owned by the user identified by {userId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the expense record.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The ID of the expense record.

definition.description: Stores income categories for a specific user. Each category is owned by the user identified by {userId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the income category.

params.name: incomeCategoryId
params.description: The ID of the income category.

definition.description: Stores expense categories for a specific user. Each category is owned by the user identified by {userId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the expense category.

params.name: expenseCategoryId
params.description: The ID of the expense category.

definition.description: Stores budget records for a specific user. Each budget is owned by the user identified by {userId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the budget.

params.name: budgetId
params.description: The ID of the budget record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to manage financial data for the ClinicFlow application, focusing on income, expenses, budgeting, and related categories. User-specific data is segregated under the `/users/{userId}` path to ensure data privacy and security. This design adheres to the principle of Authorization Independence by avoiding hierarchical authorization dependencies. All authorization rules will be based solely on the `request.auth.uid` and data present within each document, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. The structure supports QAPs (Rules are not Filters) by segregating data based on ownership and providing clear paths for data access.

Specifically:

*   **Authorization Independence:** Each document under `/users/{userId}` contains all necessary information for authorization. No `get()` calls are required to check parent document properties.
*   **Structural Segregation:** Different data types (income, expenses, budgets, categories) are stored in separate collections under the user's document, each with its own security rules.
*   **Access Modeling:** Path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`) is used to define user-specific data.
*   **QAPs:** List operations are secured by path-based rules, ensuring that users can only access data under their own user ID.

This structure enables simple, robust, and easily debuggable security rules.
